places.name: Panteón de Belen (Santa Paula Cemetery)
places.description: This historic cemetery built for victims of the epidemics that swept Guadalajara is shrouded in macabre tales.

places.name: Parque Mirador Independencia Amphitheater (Independence Lookout Park)
places.description: This concrete amphitheater offers an unforgettable view of one of Mexico's most beautiful canyons.

places.name: Casa ITESO Clavigero
places.description: This singular Mexican structure is considered a masterpiece of the Tapatía architectural school.

places.name: Museo Regional de la Cerámica (Regional Museum of Ceramics)
places.description: One of Mexico’s historic pottery-producing towns is home to a museum showcasing the craft.

places.name: Hospicio Cabañas
places.description: One of the oldest hospitals in the Americas is now decorated in hallucinatory murals.

places.name: Telmex Building
places.description: This structure was moved nearly 40 feet while people continued working inside it.

places.name: 'Sincretismo'
places.description: A beautiful and controversial modern sculpture that alludes to the Aztec past identity of the Virgin of Guadalupe and the phenomenon of syncretism.
